message:
perf(btree): gate cursor-op observability counters behind AtomicBool

`with_btree_op` calls `record_operation` (per-op `fetch_add` on one of
three BTREE_OP_*_TOTAL atomics) and `finish_btree_op_postlude` →
`record_depth_gauge` → `set_depth_gauge` (per-op store on
BTREE_DEPTH_GAUGE) on every cursor Seek/Insert/Delete. Production reads
none of these — only `fsqlite-e2e/src/fsqlite_executor.rs`'s
HotPathMetricsCapture and a handful of observability tests do — and
under MT load the unconditional cross-core `lock xadd` / store causes
cache-line ping-pong on the shared statics.

Mirror the MVCC pattern (bc4fa6b5 / d2156302 / f2707d1a / 03c49886): add
`FSQLITE_BTREE_METRICS_ENABLED: AtomicBool` (default off) and gate
`record_operation`, `set_depth_gauge`, `record_split_event`,
`record_no_split_reuse_hit`, `record_conservative_reload_fallback`, and
`record_page_header_rebuild` behind it. Each becomes "load shared bool;
branch" instead of "fetch_add to contended line." Cold body factored
into `record_operation_cold` (`#[cold]`, `#[inline(never)]`) so the
fast-path inlines into a single load+jcc pair.

`HotPathMetricsCapture::new`/`Drop` flips the gate alongside the
existing parse/vdbe gates. Eight tests that read these counters
(2 in `instrumentation::tests`, 6 in `cursor::tests`) now acquire a
shared `BTREE_METRICS_TEST_LOCK` mutex and flip the gate explicitly,
since the gate is process-global.

Microbench (`bench_btree_metrics_gate_per_op_cost`, 50M iterations,
single thread, uncontended):

    gate-off = 1.81 ns/pair
    gate-on  = 3.10 ns/pair       (-41.6% per cursor op)

The single-thread number understates the win — the actual benefit comes
from eliminating cross-core invalidation on the four shared atomic
statics under MT load (the same shape the MVCC gates landed at -7% to
-87% on their respective hot paths).

Pre-existing flaky test
`prop_index_btree_structural_invariants_hold_after_random_mutations`
also fails on main; not introduced here.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
